name: Chocolate Mosaic Fruit Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup cold butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------CHOCOLATE TART FILLING---------------------------
1/2 cup (113g) softened butter
1/2 cup powdered sugar
1 egg
4 ounces Ghirardelli Semi-Sweet chocolate
1 tablespoon heavy whipping cream
1 teaspoon vanilla or Grand Marnier
Fresh fruit: strawberries, kiwi, bananas, green grapes, oranges, etc.
1/4 cup apricot jam
Servings: 8
For crust: Break chocolate pieces into the bowl of a processor or blender. Process for about 20 to 25 seconds until the mixture resembles powder. Add flour, butter, and salt. Pulse in a food processor for 15 to 20 pulses, or combine by hand using a pastry cutter, until the butter is very fine. Stir in egg and vanilla; process for another 15 times or stir lightly until the dough holds together when pinched with fingers. Form into a ball on plastic wrap and flatten to fit an 8-inch tart pan with 1-inch fluted sides.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) in the center of the oven for 15 to 18 minutes, or until the baked shell springs back when touched. Do not overbake. Cool. Remove outer ring of pan. The crust can be prepared up to one day in advance.
Chocolate tart filling: In a small mixer bowl, beat butter with powdered sugar until fluffy. Melt chocolate in a double boiler, stirring constantly, or microwave on medium for 2½ to 3 minutes. Add the warm chocolate to the creamed mixture and beat well. Stir in heavy whipping cream and vanilla; beat until thick and creamy. Spread filling over baked Chocolate Tart Crust. While filling is still soft, arrange fruit artistically on top. For a fruit glaze, strain apricot jam with a fine-mesh sieve. Brush glaze onto the fruit pieces and the top edge of the crust. Chill for about 2 hours before serving. Decorate with chocolate curls if desired. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes before serving.
